Package com.wip.config

Class SecurityConfig

java.lang.Object
com.wip.config.SecurityConfig
@Configuration public class SecurityConfig extends Object
Spring Security configuration for the Courier Tracking System.

This class configures the complete security posture of the application using Spring Security 6. It defines the authentication mechanism via a DaoAuthenticationProvider backed by CustomUserDetailsService, sets up BCrypt-based password encoding, and establishes the HTTP security filter chain with:

  • Role-based URL authorization distinguishing ADMIN and USER roles.
  • Form-login with a custom login page at /login and redirect to / on success.
  • Session invalidation, authentication clearing, and JSESSIONID cookie deletion on logout.
  • CSRF protection disabled only for REST API endpoints under /api/**.
  • HTTP Basic authentication enabled as a fallback for API clients.
Since:
1.0
Version:
1.0
Author:
Dharshan K S (dharshan.ks@wipro.com)

  • Constructor Details

    • SecurityConfig

      public SecurityConfig(CustomUserDetailsService customUserDetailsService)
      Constructs a new SecurityConfig with the required CustomUserDetailsService.

      Spring automatically injects the CustomUserDetailsService bean into this constructor through constructor-based dependency injection.

      Parameters:
      customUserDetailsService - the service responsible for loading user details from the data store during the authentication process; must not be null

  • Method Details

    • authenticationProvider

      @Bean public org.springframework.security.authentication.dao.DaoAuthenticationProvider authenticationProvider()
      Creates and configures a DaoAuthenticationProvider bean.

      The DaoAuthenticationProvider integrates with CustomUserDetailsService to retrieve user credentials and role information from the database, and uses the passwordEncoder() bean to verify the supplied raw password against the stored BCrypt-encoded hash during the authentication process.

      Returns:
      a fully configured DaoAuthenticationProvider that delegates user lookup to CustomUserDetailsService and password verification to the BCryptPasswordEncoder

    • securityFilterChain

      @Bean public org.springframework.security.web.SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.builders.HttpSecurity http) throws Exception
      Builds and returns the primary SecurityFilterChain bean for the application.

      This method configures the HTTP security rules in the following order:

      1. Authentication Provider: Registers the DaoAuthenticationProvider.
      2. CSRF: Disabled for all paths matching /api/** to support stateless REST API clients; enabled for all other paths.
      3. Authorization:
        • Public paths (login, register, static resources, Swagger UI, Actuator) are accessible without authentication.
        • Dashboard, profile, parcels, shipments (read), tracking (read), and search endpoints require either the USER or ADMIN role.
        • Customer management, shipment management (write/delete), tracking management (write/delete), and all /api/** endpoints require the ADMIN role.
        • All other requests require the user to be authenticated.
      4. Form Login: Custom login page at /login, processing URL at /login, and redirects to / on successful authentication.
      5. Logout: Logout URL at /logout; on success, redirects to /login?logout, invalidates the HTTP session, clears authentication, and deletes the JSESSIONID cookie.
      6. HTTP Basic: Enabled with default settings for API consumers.
      Parameters:
      http - the HttpSecurity object provided by Spring Security to configure the security filter chain; must not be null
      Returns:
      the built SecurityFilterChain that enforces all configured security rules
      Throws:
      Exception - if an error occurs while building the HttpSecurity configuration

    • passwordEncoder

      @Bean public org.springframework.security.crypto.password.PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder()
      Creates and returns a PasswordEncoder bean backed by the BCrypt hashing algorithm.

      BCrypt is a strong, adaptive, salted hashing algorithm recommended by OWASP for password storage. The encoder automatically generates a random salt and embeds it in the resulting hash, making each encoded password unique even for identical raw inputs. This bean is used both by the authenticationProvider() during login verification and by the registration service when persisting new user passwords.

      Returns:
      a BCryptPasswordEncoder instance configured with Spring Security's default BCrypt strength factor (10 rounds)
